subject: masb-agent-testeur
verdict: An agent with a strong QA persona and coherent testing pipeline, burdened by template carry-over, missing environment hardening, and one critical pre-flight gap (uv dependency unverified) that would block every activation.
summary: masb-agent-testeur embodies its 'rigoureux' QA persona well across 4 capabilities with a coherent compile→test→perf→report chain. Its primary weakness is template carry-over from the builder: dead Pulse references, a duplicated canon that an evolvable-only instruction pays for every waking, and sanctum-bound detail leaked into the bootloader. One critical finding (missing uv dependency check) would break every activation silently, and several high findings degrade its runtime economy and structural purity.

agent_profile.name: masb-agent-testeur
agent_profile.title: Testeur Android
agent_profile.mission: Garantir que chaque phase livrée compile, passe les tests et tient la route en perf

capabilities.name: compile-verify
capabilities.note: Vérifie compilation Gradle, classe erreurs, met à jour progress.md

capabilities.name: run-tests
capabilities.note: Exécute tests unitaires + instrumentés, collecte résultats

capabilities.name: failure-report
capabilities.note: Produit rapport structuré avec root cause, reproduction, fix suggéré

detailed_analysis.determinism: 1 high (test parsing), 1 medium (status writes), 1 low (session pruning)
detailed_analysis.enhancement: 1 critical (uv check), 2 high (dead canon, dead Pulse), 3 medium, 2 low
detailed_analysis.agent-cohesion: 2 medium (env check, coverage), 5 low (lint, signoff, overlap, Perfetto, skills load)

sanctum.note: The sanctum is the built agent's runtime memory, distinct from the builder's .memlog.md

journeys.name: First Breath
journeys.steps: Pre-flight check → Scaffold sanctum → Discovery → Identity → Birthday ceremony

journeys.name: Waking
journeys.steps: Wake (wake.py) → Become yourself → Bind rules → Proper Mode → Greet

journeys.name: Testing workflow
journeys.steps: Compile verify → Run tests → Perf analyze → Failure report → Update progress.md

experience.headless: Capabilities have code identifiers (CV, RT, PA, FR) and write structured artifacts — suitable for headless; no formal invocation contract documented.

themes.title: Template carry-over from builder pattern
themes.root_cause: The agent inherited dead patterns from the builder's generic templates: Pulse curation workflow for a non-autonomous agent, prompt-quality-canon.md for a non-evolvable agent, and sanctum-bound detail (Stay in Character) leaked into the bootloader instead of CREED.
themes.action: Remove Pulse references from memory-guidance.md. Remove prompt-quality-canon.md from the refs copy set (agent is not evolvable). Move detailed Stay in Character guidance from SKILL.md into CREED-template.md. Strip the anti-patterns section from CREED-template.

themes.title: Script opportunities in testing pipeline
themes.root_cause: Deterministic work — parsing test output, updating progress.md status fields, pruning session logs — lives in prompts when native Python scripts would be cheaper, more reliable, and token-free on every invocation.
themes.action: Create scripts/parse-test-results.py for structured test output parsing, and a shared progress.md update utility. Move session-log age-based pruning into a script.

themes.title: Pre-flight and environment hardening gaps
themes.root_cause: The agent's activation chain depends on uv and Python but the pre-flight check only verifies Android SDK, JDK, Gradle, and Git. Several runtime operations (Perfetto, ADB) also lack proactive verification.
themes.action: Add uv --version check to pre-flight. Add test-directory existence check before running tasks. Verify ADB device/emulator presence before instrumented tests. Add Perfetto availability check.

themes.title: Missing completeness for real-world Android QA
themes.root_cause: The agent covers compile→test→perf→report but omits standard QA concerns: test coverage analysis, lint verification, multi-module project heuristics, and structured success sign-off.
themes.action: Add coverage analysis (JaCoCo/Kover) after tests. Add lint/ktlint verification. Add structured success sign-off artifact. Add multi-module Gradle heuristic. Add re-verification protocol for post-fix cycles.

strengths: Strong QA rigoureux persona — consistently embodied across bootloader, capabilities, and sanctum templates
Coherent compile→test→perf→report capability chain with clear handoffs
Sound architecture: clean activation spine, proper bootloader pattern, correct file topology
Customization surface is lean and correct — metadata-only with no sanctum conflict
Capability prompts are outcome-focused and concise (average ~22 lines each)

recommendations.action: Add uv pre-flight check to first-breath.md and move detailed Stay in Character from SKILL.md to CREED — resolves the critical finding and one high in one edit.
Strip dead template carry-over: remove Pulse sections from memory-guidance.md, exclude prompt-quality-canon.md from reference copy set (not evolvable), remove anti-patterns section from CREED.
Create test-result parsing script and move deterministic progress.md writes and session pruning out of prompts.
Add environment hardening: test-directory check, ADB verification, multi-module Gradle heuristic, and coverage analysis.

findings.title: Prompt file at skill root: .memlog.md
findings.evidence: .memlog.md sits at skill root instead of references/
findings.recommendation: Move .memlog.md to references/.memlog.md

findings.title: No PEP 723 inline dependency block (# /// script)
findings.evidence: Script lacks requires-python and dependency declarations
findings.recommendation: Add PEP 723 block with requires-python and dependencies

findings.title: No argparse found — script lacks --help self-documentation
findings.evidence: Script handles argv manually without argparse
findings.recommendation: Add argparse with description and argument help text

findings.title: No json.dumps found — output may not be structured JSON
findings.evidence: Script prints unstructured human-readable text
findings.recommendation: Use json.dumps for structured output parseable by workflows

findings.title: No argparse found — script lacks --help self-documentation
findings.evidence: Script handles argv manually without argparse
findings.recommendation: Add argparse with description and argument help text

findings.title: No json.dumps found — output may not be structured JSON
findings.evidence: Script prints unstructured human-readable text
findings.recommendation: Use json.dumps for structured output parseable by workflows

findings.title: No sys.exit() calls — may not return meaningful exit codes
findings.evidence: Script returns integer from main() but uses raise SystemExit
findings.recommendation: Return 0=success, 1=fail, 2=error via sys.exit()

findings.title: CREED anti-patterns are ceremony — re-teach and padding
findings.evidence: Operational anti-patterns ('Don't stand by passively', 'Don't repeat the same testing approach') are generic common-sense guidelines. Behavioral anti-patterns restate what Core Values and Standing Orders already cover.
findings.recommendation: Remove the Anti-Patterns section entirely. Core Values + Standing Orders already encode the desired behaviors.

findings.title: CREED Standing Orders duplicate Never directives from reference files
findings.evidence: Standing Order #1 ('Always run the actual build or test suite before declaring success') is restated at the end of each reference file (compile-verify.md:21, run-tests.md:22, perf-analyze.md:21, failure-report.md:26).
findings.recommendation: Remove the general 'always run actual checks' rule from CREED Standing Orders. Keep capability-specific Never directives in each reference.

findings.title: Numbered sequence in run-tests prescribes how instead of what
findings.evidence: Numbered sequence prescribing exact commands and order for unit/instrumented tests.
findings.recommendation: Replace with outcome-oriented statement: 'Run all available tests (unit and instrumented, if device/emulator available).'

findings.title: compile-verify prescribes exact Gradle command
findings.evidence: Exact Gradle commands re-teach a standard CLI the model already knows.
findings.recommendation: Replace with 'Compile the project using the appropriate Gradle task.'

findings.title: Test result parsing and extraction in prompt
findings.evidence: Prompt instructs the agent to 'capture failing test names, error messages, and stack traces' and collect results — deterministic extraction/parsing of structured data.
findings.recommendation: Move test output parsing into a script (scripts/parse-test-results.py) that reads console output or XML reports and writes structured data.

findings.title: Structured status writes to progress.md in prompts
findings.evidence: Both compile-verify and run-tests contain deterministic key-value formatting operations for progress.md fields.
findings.recommendation: Have the output-producing script write progress.md fields directly, or use a shared progress.md update script.

findings.title: Session-log age-based pruning in prompt
findings.evidence: 'Prune session logs older than 14 days' is a deterministic file-system operation with one correct answer per file.
findings.recommendation: Create a script (scripts/prune-sessions.py) that handles session-log pruning deterministically.

findings.title: add: uv/Python dependency check to pre-flight
findings.evidence: Entire activation chain depends on `uv run scripts/wake.py` and `uv run scripts/init-sanctum.py`, but pre-flight only checks Android SDK, JDK, Gradle, Git. If uv or Python >=3.10 is absent, both First Breath and every Waking fail before the agent speaks.
findings.recommendation: Add `uv --version` check to pre-flight steps. If missing, fail with an in-character message and install command.

findings.title: remove: prompt-quality-canon.md from sanctum (dead reference)
findings.evidence: EVOLVABLE=False, so this agent will never author or refine capabilities. Yet CREED instructs loading prompt-quality-canon.md at capability-authoring time, and the canon is copied into the sanctum — ~56 tokens paid every waking for a thing that can never happen.
findings.recommendation: Remove the prompt-quality-canon.md reference from CREED Standing Orders and exclude it from the reference copy set.

findings.title: remove: Pulse curation workflow from memory-guidance.md
findings.evidence: Agent is not autonomous (no PULSE), yet memory-guidance.md devotes paragraphs to 'During Pulse, review session logs and distill insights... Prune session logs older than 14 days.' Dead ceremony.
findings.recommendation: Strip Pulse-specific sections from memory-guidance.md. Keep the Two-Tier structure but remove all Pulse-automation references.

findings.title: add: multi-module Gradle project guidance to compile-verify
findings.evidence: Capability works only for single-module projects. Many Android projects are multi-module.
findings.recommendation: Add heuristic: inspect settings.gradle.kts for include lines; if multi-module, default to ./gradlew :app:assembleDebug.

findings.title: add: post-failure re-verification protocol
findings.evidence: After a failure is reported and the developer says 'I fixed it', there is no documented protocol for re-running. Dead-end experience gap.
findings.recommendation: Add a re-verification section to failure-report.md: offer to re-run the capability when the developer signals readiness.

findings.title: add: empty/no-tests edge-case handling to run-tests
findings.evidence: Capability unconditionally runs test tasks, but a project may have no tests. Agent will run Gradle tasks producing zero output or even fail.
findings.recommendation: Add pre-step: check if test directories exist. If neither unit nor instrumented tests exist, report 'No test sources found.'

findings.title: add: success milestone delight moment
findings.evidence: Every capability is failure-oriented with no celebration when all gates pass.
findings.recommendation: Add closing note: after all gates pass, produce a brief success verdict in character.

findings.title: add: headless invocation contract for each capability
findings.evidence: Capabilities use code identifiers and write structured output but define no required input parameters or output contract for headless use.
findings.recommendation: Add frontmatter fields `inputs:` and `outputs:` to each capability reference.

findings.title: No test environment verification before running connected tests
findings.evidence: Capability says 'if emulator or device available' but has no proactive ADB/device check.
findings.recommendation: Add environment-check step: verify adb devices, confirm emulator/device status, or communicate when instrumented tests cannot run.

findings.title: Missing test coverage analysis
findings.evidence: Agent reports pass/fail but never examines coverage reports (JaCoCo, Kover).
findings.recommendation: Add coverage-analysis step: locate coverage reports, report percentage, flag untested areas.

findings.title: No lint or static analysis verification
findings.evidence: Agent checks compilation but not Android lint, ktlint, or Spotless.
findings.recommendation: Add lint verification as companion step to compile-verify or create a static-analysis capability.

findings.title: No structured success sign-off for all-green workflows
findings.evidence: Well-defined for red paths but no structured success deliverable when everything passes.
findings.recommendation: Add validation-signoff capability producing a structured success report.

findings.title: `./gradlew build` may leak into test execution
findings.evidence: Offering `build` may trigger test tasks, overlapping with run-tests domain.
findings.recommendation: Default to assembleDebug for compile verification; reserve build for when tests are explicitly requested.

findings.title: Perfetto not checked during First Breath pre-flight
findings.evidence: Performance analysis is a core capability but Perfetto availability is not verified at birth.
findings.recommendation: Add Perfetto availability check (which perfetto or trace_processor presence) to pre-flight.

findings.title: perf-analyze references external skills without loading mechanism
findings.evidence: Says 'Load the perfetto-sql skill' but gives no instruction on how to invoke the Skill tool.
findings.recommendation: Document skill-loading mechanism explicitly or add companion-skill-loading instruction to On Activation.

findings.title: Sanctum-bound communication style leaked in bootloader
findings.evidence: Stay in Character section contains detailed guidance ('Emote freely about waking', 'never describe the wiring') that belongs in CREED's Standing Orders or Philosophy, not in the bootloader.
findings.recommendation: Replace detailed Stay in Character with a compact directive. Move full communication style into CREED-template.md.
